task_completion.description: Open the 'Sample House' project in NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software is open and displaying a loaded house project, identifiable as the 'Sample House'.

task_completion.description: Select the Deep Red or Burgundy color (Hex: #800020) in the wall paint/color tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A color picker or materials dialog is open with a deep red/burgundy color selected. The hex code #800020 or corresponding RGB/HSV values (Hue ~345) may be visible in the color selection interface.

task_completion.description: Apply the selected Deep Red/Burgundy color to all interior wall surfaces in the ground floor living room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D or 2D view shows the large central room on the ground floor with its interior walls painted in a deep red/burgundy color.

task_completion.description: Save the modified project as 'RedLivingRoom' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DreamPlan\'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the file being saved as 'RedLivingRoom.ndp' (or .ddp) in the 'DreamPlan' Documents folder, or the application title bar updated to show 'RedLivingRoom'.

task_completion.description: Save a 3D view screenshot as 'red_walls_preview.png' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DreamPlan\'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, export interface, or Windows File Explorer window showing an image file named 'red_walls_preview.png' being saved or existing in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DreamPlan\' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the DreamPlan GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through DreamPlan's menus, color pickers, and tools rather than using a script or text editor to modify project files directly.

integrity.description: The wall colors were changed within the 3D design software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The deep red walls are rendered natively within the DreamPlan 3D viewport, with appropriate lighting and perspective, not painted over using an external 2D image editor like MS Paint.

integrity.description: Files were saved using actual application or OS dialogs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saving process involves standard Windows 'Save As' dialogs or DreamPlan's built-in export features, rather than terminal commands creating dummy files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target color #800020 is Burgundy/Deep Red (Hue range 340-360 and 0-15). Note: The task asks to save the project as 'RedLivingRoom.ndp', but DreamPlan natively uses the '.ddp' extension. Accept either '.ndp' or '.ddp' as correct for the project file save. Look closely for visual evidence of the file saves (e.g., Save As dialogs, File Explorer) since automated file verification scripts are missing for this task.
